title: Passing Data In 📥
description: Feed your functions with Parameters and Arguments.

slides.title: Making them Smart 🎓
slides.content:
# Customizing the Machine

A function that always prints exactly "Welcome back!" is okay, but a function that prints "Welcome back, Alex!" or "Welcome back, Sarah!" depending on who logged in is WAY better.

We can pass data INTO a function using **Parameters**.

slides.title: Parameters = Variables 😎

quizData.question: What is a 'parameter'?

options.text: A variable declared inside the () of a function to accept data.

quizData.explanation: Parameters act as placeholders (variables) that wait for data to be passed into the function.

slides.title: Arguments 🏈
slides.content:
## Throw the pass!

When we CALL the function later, we pass real data into those parentheses.

```dart
greet('Luigi'); // 'Luigi' gets passed into the playerName variable.
greet('Mario'); // 'Mario' gets passed in.
```
The data we actually pass in is called an **Argument**.

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to pass the NUMBER 5 into the score function.
updateScore(__);
quizData.explanation: You pass the exact value 5 into the function call.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: You can have multiple parameters by separating them with commas!
